**Johny Hendricks** (born September 12, 1983) is an American former [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") and [collegiate wrestler](/wiki/Collegiate_wrestling "Collegiate wrestling"). He competed in the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") and [middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight_(MMA) "Middleweight (MMA)") divisions of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC) and is a former [UFC Welterweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Welterweight_Championship "UFC Welterweight Championship").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section of a [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **needs additional [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). [Contentious material](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons#Remove_contentious_material_that_is_unsourced_or_poorly_sourced "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") about living persons that is unsourced or [poorly sourced](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources#Questionable_and_self-published_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources") **must be removed immediately** from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Johny Hendricks"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Johny+Hendricks%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Johny+Hendricks%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Johny+Hendricks%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Johny+Hendricks%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Johny+Hendricks%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Johny+Hendricks%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Hendricks was born in [Ada, Oklahoma](/wiki/Ada,_Oklahoma "Ada, Oklahoma"). He was a three-time [Oklahoma](/wiki/Oklahoma "Oklahoma") high school [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") state champion and was a two-time high school national champion while at [Edmond Memorial High School](/wiki/Edmond_Memorial_High_School "Edmond Memorial High School"). His final high school record was 101–5. He would go on to attend college at [Oklahoma State University](/wiki/Oklahoma_State_University "Oklahoma State University") (OSU).

After a 10–0 redshirt season in 2003, Hendricks finished 5th in the nation at 157 pounds in 2004, while compiling a 37–7 record. Hendricks moved up to 165 pounds for the next three seasons. He won the 2005 and 2006 [Big 12](/wiki/Big_12_Conference "Big 12 Conference") titles and [NCAA Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") national championships.[[5]](#cite_note-TTD_Info-5) He was 27–4 in 2005 and 29–1 in 2006.

Going into the 2007 national championships final match, Hendricks had a record of 34–0 for the season and had won his third straight Big 12 title along the way.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He suffered an upset loss to [Mark Perry](/wiki/Iowa_Hawkeyes#Wrestling "Iowa Hawkeyes") from the [University of Iowa](/wiki/University_of_Iowa "University of Iowa") and finished 2nd in the nation. The four-time [All-American](/wiki/All-American "All-American") for OSU finished with a college record of 159–13.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

After graduating from [OSU](/wiki/Oklahoma_State_University%E2%80%93Stillwater "Oklahoma State University–Stillwater") in 2007, Hendricks moved to Las Vegas and trained at Cobra Kai Jiu Jitsu under Marc Laimon,[[5]](#cite_note-TTD_Info-5) along with two other former OSU wrestlers, [Shane Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller") and [Jake Rosholt](/wiki/Jake_Rosholt "Jake Rosholt"). Hendricks then later moved to Texas and signed with Team Takedown. This elite fighter management team funds fighters' training and splits his profits.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Hendricks would go on to have his first career mixed martial arts (MMA) fight only seven months after his last collegiate wrestling match. He made his professional MMA debut on September 28, 2007, in which he scored a third-round controversial [TKO](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") victory over Victor Rackliff at Masters of the Cage 16 held in [Oklahoma City, Oklahoma](/wiki/Oklahoma_City,_Oklahoma "Oklahoma City, Oklahoma"). Rackliff was getting the better of the exchanges on the feet and he rocked Hendricks in the 3rd when both fighters exchanged punches.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Hendricks fought once more in 2007, defeating Spencer Cowley by second-round TKO at Snakebite Fight Night held in [Tulsa, Oklahoma](/wiki/Tulsa,_Oklahoma "Tulsa, Oklahoma").[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Hendricks won his television debut on March 15, 2008, with a first-round submission victory over Richard Gamble at [HDNet Fights](/wiki/HDNet_Fights "HDNet Fights") Xtreme Fighting League, also in [Tulsa](/wiki/Tulsa,_Oklahoma "Tulsa, Oklahoma").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### World Extreme Cagefighting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: World Extreme Cagefighting")]

Hendricks, [Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller") and [Rosholt](/wiki/Jake_Rosholt "Jake Rosholt") signed multi-fight deals with [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") (WEC).[[12]](#cite_note-12)

While in the WEC, Hendricks defeated Justin Haskins on December 3, 2008, at [WEC 37](/wiki/WEC_37 "WEC 37").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Next, Hendricks defeated [Alex Serdyukov](/wiki/Alex_Serdyukov "Alex Serdyukov") via unanimous decision on March 1, 2009, at [WEC 39](/wiki/WEC_39 "WEC 39").[[14]](#cite_note-14) which was the last 170-pound bout in WEC history due to the company's decision to dissolve its welterweight division and focus on lighter weight fighters.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

With the WEC eliminating his weight class, Hendricks had to find another place to fight and signed with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). In May 2009, it was announced that Hendricks would be making his [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") debut against [Ultimate Fighter 7](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_7 "The Ultimate Fighter 7") champion [Amir Sadollah](/wiki/Amir_Sadollah "Amir Sadollah") at [UFC 101](/wiki/UFC_101 "UFC 101") in [Philadelphia, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Philadelphia,_Pennsylvania "Philadelphia, Pennsylvania"), on August 8, 2009.[[16]](#cite_note-16) In the opening seconds of the first round, Hendricks blocked a high head kick from Sadollah and countered with an uppercut, knocking Sadollah to his knees. Hendricks continued to land punches until referee [Dan Miragliotta](/wiki/Dan_Miragliotta "Dan Miragliotta") pulled him off and ended the fight 29 seconds into the first round. Some people criticized Miragliotta for stopping the fight too early, as Sadollah seemed to be trying to get up, but Miragliotta defended his stoppage, saying Sadollah was "out of it" and "still looked glassy eyed and asked me what happened."[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Hendricks faced promotional newcomer [Ricardo Funch](/wiki/Ricardo_Funch "Ricardo Funch") on December 12, 2009, at [UFC 107](/wiki/UFC_107 "UFC 107").[[18]](#cite_note-18) Hendricks won the fight via unanimous decision (30–27, 30–27, and 30–25).

Hendricks next faced [T. J. Grant](/wiki/T._J._Grant "T. J. Grant") on May 8, 2010, at [UFC 113](/wiki/UFC_113 "UFC 113").[[19]](#cite_note-19) Hendricks won the bout via majority decision, improving his record to 3–0 in the UFC.

He next faced [Charlie Brenneman](/wiki/Charlie_Brenneman "Charlie Brenneman") on August 7, 2010, at [UFC 117](/wiki/UFC_117 "UFC 117").[[20]](#cite_note-20) Hendricks defeated him via TKO in the second round.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Hendricks fought [Rick Story](/wiki/Rick_Story "Rick Story") on December 4, 2010, at [The Ultimate Fighter 12 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale").[[22]](#cite_note-22) Hendricks lost via unanimous decision.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

#### Win streak

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Win streak")]

Hendricks was expected to face [Paulo Thiago](/wiki/Paulo_Thiago "Paulo Thiago") on March 3, 2011, at [UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Sanchez_vs._Kampmann "UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann").[[24]](#cite_note-24) However, Thiago was forced out of the bout with an elbow injury.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Instead, Hendricks fought [TJ Waldburger](/wiki/TJ_Waldburger "TJ Waldburger") on March 26, 2011, at [UFC Fight Night 24](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_24 "UFC Fight Night 24"), replacing an injured [Dennis Hallman](/wiki/Dennis_Hallman "Dennis Hallman").[[26]](#cite_note-26) Hendricks won via first-round TKO, earning *Knockout of the Night* honors.[[27]](#cite_note-UFN24-27)

Hendricks faced [Mike Pierce](/wiki/Mike_Pierce "Mike Pierce") on August 6, 2011, at [UFC 133](/wiki/UFC_133 "UFC 133").[[28]](#cite_note-28) Hendricks won by split decision.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

Hendricks fought longtime #2 welterweight [Jon Fitch](/wiki/Jon_Fitch "Jon Fitch") on December 30, 2011, at [UFC 141](/wiki/UFC_141 "UFC 141").[[30]](#cite_note-30) Hendricks became the first man to finish Fitch in the UFC, winning via knockout just 12 seconds into the first round.[[31]](#cite_note-31) The performance also earned *Knockout of the Night* honors.[[32]](#cite_note-UFC141-32)

Hendricks next faced [Josh Koscheck](/wiki/Josh_Koscheck "Josh Koscheck") on May 5, 2012, in the co-main event at [UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Diaz_vs._Miller "UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller").[[33]](#cite_note-33) He won the fight via split decision.

Hendricks faced [Martin Kampmann](/wiki/Martin_Kampmann "Martin Kampmann") on November 17, 2012, at [UFC 154](/wiki/UFC_154 "UFC 154").[[34]](#cite_note-34) Hendricks won via KO in the first round, with the fight only lasting 46 seconds. He earned *Knockout of the Night* honors for his performance.[[35]](#cite_note-35)[[36]](#cite_note-UFC154-36)

Hendricks was expected to face [Jake Ellenberger](/wiki/Jake_Ellenberger "Jake Ellenberger") on March 16, 2013, at [UFC 158](/wiki/UFC_158 "UFC 158").[[37]](#cite_note-37) However, Hendricks was pulled from the Ellenberger bout in favor of a matchup with [Carlos Condit](/wiki/Carlos_Condit "Carlos Condit") at the event after Condit's original opponent, [Rory MacDonald](/wiki/Rory_MacDonald_(fighter) "Rory MacDonald (fighter)"), pulled out of the bout with an injury.[[38]](#cite_note-38) Hendricks won the fight via unanimous decision and both participants earned *Fight of the Night* honors.[[39]](#cite_note-UFC158-39)

#### Welterweight title bouts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Welterweight title bouts")]

Having earned his title shot, Hendricks faced [Georges St-Pierre](/wiki/Georges_St-Pierre "Georges St-Pierre") on November 16, 2013, at [UFC 167](/wiki/UFC_167 "UFC 167") for the [UFC Welterweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Welterweight_Championship "UFC Welterweight Championship").[[40]](#cite_note-40) He lost via controversial split decision, a loss that UFC president [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") felt was unwarranted as he publicly stated he believed Hendricks won the fight,[[41]](#cite_note-41) though White later reportedly told St-Pierre that he had indeed won.[[42]](#cite_note-42) In addition, the site MMADecisions.com reported that every one of the sixteen MMA media members whose scores they collect declared the fight a decision victory for Hendricks.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Despite the controversy, the bout did earn him *Fight of the Night* honors in the UFC for the second time.[[44]](#cite_note-UFC167-44)

On December 13, 2013, news came that [Georges St-Pierre](/wiki/Georges_St-Pierre "Georges St-Pierre") would be taking an indefinite break from MMA and vacating his belt.[[45]](#cite_note-45) Following this report, [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") announced that Hendricks would be facing [Robbie Lawler](/wiki/Robbie_Lawler "Robbie Lawler") on March 15, 2014, at [UFC 171](/wiki/UFC_171 "UFC 171") for the vacant [UFC Welterweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Welterweight_Championship "UFC Welterweight Championship").[[46]](#cite_note-46) Hendricks won the back-and-forth fight via unanimous decision. The bout also earned him his third consecutive *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[47]](#cite_note-UFC171-47) Hendricks underwent surgery to repair a torn bicep that he sustained prior to his fight with Lawler. He returned to training in May 2014.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

A rematch with [Robbie Lawler](/wiki/Robbie_Lawler "Robbie Lawler") took place at [UFC 181](/wiki/UFC_181 "UFC 181") on December 6, 2014.[[49]](#cite_note-49) After a strong start, Hendricks slowed in the later rounds and ultimately lost the fight and the title via split decision.[[50]](#cite_note-50)

#### Post-championship fights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Post-championship fights")]

Hendricks faced [Matt Brown](/wiki/Matt_Brown_(fighter) "Matt Brown (fighter)") on March 14, 2015, at [UFC 185](/wiki/UFC_185 "UFC 185").[[51]](#cite_note-51) While Brown had a limited amount of success on the feet, Hendricks was successful on nine of ten takedown attempts and neutralized Brown's attacks. Hendricks won the fight via unanimous decision.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

Hendricks was expected to face [Tyron Woodley](/wiki/Tyron_Woodley "Tyron Woodley") on October 3, 2015, at [UFC 192](/wiki/UFC_192 "UFC 192").[[53]](#cite_note-53) However, the bout was scrapped prior to the weigh-ins due to an intestinal blockage and [kidney stone attack](/wiki/Kidney_stone "Kidney stone") suffered by Hendricks during the weight cut.[[54]](#cite_note-54)

Hendricks faced [Stephen Thompson](/wiki/Stephen_Thompson_(fighter) "Stephen Thompson (fighter)") on February 6, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 82](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_82 "UFC Fight Night 82").[[55]](#cite_note-55) Despite being the betting favorite in this encounter, Hendricks had no answer for Thompson's striking and lost the bout via TKO in the first round, marking the first time Hendricks had ever been finished in his MMA career.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

Hendricks faced [Kelvin Gastelum](/wiki/Kelvin_Gastelum "Kelvin Gastelum") on July 9, 2016, at [UFC 200](/wiki/UFC_200 "UFC 200").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[58]](#cite_note-58) Prior to the bout, Hendricks missed weight by a quarter of a pound and therefore surrendered 20% of his purse to Gastelum.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

Hendricks faced [Neil Magny](/wiki/Neil_Magny "Neil Magny") on December 30, 2016, at [UFC 207](/wiki/UFC_207 "UFC 207").[[60]](#cite_note-60) At the weigh-ins, Hendricks once again missed weight, weighing in at 173.5 lbs., two and a half pounds over the [welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight "Welterweight") limit. As a result, he forfeited 20% of his purse to Magny.[[61]](#cite_note-61) Hendricks lost the bout by unanimous decision in what was a close and competitive fight.[[62]](#cite_note-62) Prior to the fight, Hendricks announced his intentions to move up to middleweight after facing Magny due to the hard weight-cut.[[63]](#cite_note-63)

#### Middleweight

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Middleweight")]

After several struggles to make the welterweight limit, Hendricks opted to move up a weight class to the middleweight division. He faced [Héctor Lombard](/wiki/H%C3%A9ctor_Lombard "Héctor Lombard") in his middleweight debut on February 19, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 105](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_105 "UFC Fight Night 105").[[64]](#cite_note-64) Hendricks won the fight via unanimous decision.[[65]](#cite_note-65)

Hendricks faced [Tim Boetsch](/wiki/Tim_Boetsch "Tim Boetsch") on June 25, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 112](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_112 "UFC Fight Night 112"). He missed weight for this event, and weighed in at 188 pounds, 2 pounds over the middleweight limit of 186 pounds for a non-championship fight.[[66]](#cite_note-66) Hendricks lost the fight via TKO due to a head kick and punches early in the second round.[[67]](#cite_note-67)

Hendricks faced [Paulo Costa](/wiki/Paulo_Costa "Paulo Costa") on November 4, 2017, at [UFC 217](/wiki/UFC_217 "UFC 217").[[68]](#cite_note-68) He lost the fight via TKO in the second round. It was the last fight on Hendricks's contract.[[69]](#cite_note-69)

On June 27, 2018, Hendricks announced his retirement from competing in professional mixed martial arts and would concentrate on coaching wrestling.[[70]](#cite_note-70)

## Bare knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Bare knuckle boxing")]

Only a few months after retiring from mixed martial arts, news surfaced that Hendricks would be transitioning into bare knuckle boxing. His debut was expected to be against fellow retired mixed martial artist [Brennan Ward](/wiki/Brennan_Ward "Brennan Ward") at World Bare Knuckle Fighting Federation's inaugural event on November 9, 2018.[[71]](#cite_note-71) During the fight week, Ward announced that he won't be competing at the event due to a contract dispute and was replaced by [Dakota Cochrane](/wiki/Dakota_Cochrane "Dakota Cochrane").[[72]](#cite_note-72)[[73]](#cite_note-73) Cochrane defeated Hendricks by knock out in the second round.[[74]](#cite_note-74)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Johny_Hendricks&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Hendricks and his wife Christina have four children.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

Hendricks has been sponsored by [Reebok](/wiki/Reebok "Reebok") since [UFC 167](/wiki/UFC_167 "UFC 167").[[76]](#cite_note-76)

Hendricks's moniker, 'Bigg Rigg', is derived from his jiu-jitsu coach's comment that Hendricks hit like a [Mack truck](/wiki/Mack_Trucks "Mack Trucks"), having also owned a [Ford](/wiki/Ford_Motor_Company "Ford Motor Company") F350 truck.[[77]](#cite_note-77)

Hendricks is of [German](/wiki/American_people_of_German_descent "American people of German descent"), [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_people "Dutch people"), and [Native American](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States") descent and a member of the [Otoe–Missouria Tribe of Indians](/wiki/Otoe%E2%80%93Missouria_Tribe_of_Indians "Otoe–Missouria Tribe of Indians"). His great great grandfather was an Otoe Missouri Tribal chief.[[78]](#cite_note-78)

After retiring from mixed martial arts competition, Hendricks began working as a police officer in Texas.[[79]](#cite_note-79)
